reviewCleaned: You are a young Bird Girl named Claire who is visiting her Aunt on an Island First your skills are very bad. You can learn to glide. Than you get your first Quest to get 15 Seashells (Mussels) You have to explore & speak with People & help them (finding a Watch, finding a Camping Permission) You find a shovel to dig on special places (Holes & 4 Treasure Maps, surely could be better used a few more Times for Hidden Stuff) You find a Water Bucket to water Flowers that grow & you can use to jump very high for Shortcuts (sometimes its tricky to water them) Than you find a PIckaxe to open Mines (4 Gates) for more Shortcuts, could be used a little more often for breaking Stones or something) You can fish a lot of rare Fishes to get Money & Highscore Main Reason is to get on the Top of the Island. To get there you need at least 7 of 20 Gold Feathers You can buy them (40-100 Coins), find some on Rocks, well hidden Golden Chests (Cliff, Light Tower, Overwatch), playing Beach Game, Boat Race & other Stuff) You can gain 2 Silver Feathers too for beeing faster at Climbing or fly higher (every Time use 1 Gold Feather for a little boost) There are 3 Races to beat too. Challenging you can only use a special amount of Gold Feathers, so you need to be quick, use many Shortcuts & find the best Way easy 100 % Steam Achievements with Guide (only 30 Points Beach Game & Races (without Silver Feathers/Shortcuts Flowers) are a little very trickynice Island Design (surely Potencial unused like Graveyard) + fine Sound + friendly good Developer Support (Steam CommunityGraphic love or hate it (for me its Art - no Five (5) Trading Cards - Controls / Camera sometimes make you unlucky fallings deep down or on Races pressing Z for Jump/ Glide & C for Sprints only played by left Hand is really not enjoyable/ easy to handle. Better change both on D & F - no Steam Cloud Saving - very short Game if you rush through get fast all 7-8 Gold Feathers go on Top & End this Game Where is Short Hike 2 on a much bigger Island???!!! Good for Achievement Hunters, relaxing Players & Indie Game Explorers Buy it with => 50 % Discount or support Developer Thx 4 reading. Hope you enjoy like me & 99 % of Review Writers. Happy Gaming. Friends ask me how I could like a "Stardew knockoff" considering I've logged over 1,000+ hours into that game. I tell them its because Mistria is not Stardew. It is its own farming sim. One that refreshes the genre 10 years after Stardew launched. The low risk high reward format of this game keeps it so cozy and sucks the player in for hours. The customization of pretty much everything keeps it fresh and limitless. We aren't pigeon holed into the need for doing EVERYTHING, we can farm if we want, raise animals if we want. The game progresses regardless from you doing what YOU want to do. Key Things That I Love Most - Male animals produce products! This makes the color breeding a much more enticing challenge. - So much to forage. You feel compelled to explore and the item sprites are always stunning so you may just choose to leave forageables to decorate the landscape. - The magic brings a uniqueness to the game that serves a purpose other than for just cutscenes or plot points. - A range of crops to grow each season as well as being able to farm the forage items. - A diverse cast of bachelors and bachelorettes, but more importantly we have INTERESTING NPCs that don't feel like filler even after 200 hours of gameplay. (I have played it on other accounts in addition to this one.) Things I Hope To See (I have not accomplished everything added since the 4th Major Update) - Our own Saturday market stand. Maybe mid to late game. - More Festivals. - More crops. - Ponds or aquariums. - More than one pet. - More spells. I cannot wait to keep playing this game and discovering what else it has in store for us all.

I only wish there were chickens in this game. What's the point of putting up fencing if there aren't any chickens? What kind of glade doesn't have chickens? I feel like I'm missing out on ambient clucking as well. Looking forward to DLC (DownLoadable Chickens) to fill the egg-shaped hole in my heart.
